metadata.title: WHO Guidelines on the Quality, Safety and Efficacy of mRNA Vaccines for the Prevention of Infectious Diseases — Annex 3

rules.rule_text: The batch formula must be provided for all components of the mRNA vaccine including lipid nanoparticle (LNP) components, active substance, and excipients.
rules.verbatim_quote: The batch formula should be provided for all components including the active substance (mRNA), lipids, and excipients used in the formulation.

rules.rule_text: Additional immunomodulators, adjuvants, additional peptides or proteins, and additional excipients must each be individually documented with respect to their chemical nature and formulation.
rules.verbatim_quote: The chemical nature and formulation should be described for additional immunomodulators or adjuvants, additional peptides or proteins, and additional excipients.

rules.rule_text: The quality, provenance, and source of LNP lipids must be documented, with additional controls applied to novel lipids not previously used in licensed vaccines.
rules.source_section: 6.3 Control of starting and raw materials and excipients
rules.verbatim_quote: The quality and provenance of the LNP lipids should be documented. For novel lipids not used previously in licensed vaccines, additional controls are required.
rules.exceptions: Established lipids with existing pharmacopoeial monographs may use those standards

rules.rule_text: A nitrosamine risk assessment must be performed for all starting and raw materials and excipients used in mRNA vaccine manufacture.
rules.source_section: 6.3 Control of starting and raw materials and excipients
rules.verbatim_quote: A nitrosamine risk assessment should be performed for the starting and raw materials and excipients used in the manufacture of the mRNA vaccine.

rules.rule_text: PEGylated lipid controls must be established and documented, including controls for PEG content and PEG molecular weight distribution.
rules.source_section: 6.3 Control of starting and raw materials and excipients
rules.verbatim_quote: Controls for PEGylated lipids should be established, including controls for PEG content and PEG molecular weight distribution.

rules.rule_text: For linear DNA derived from plasmid used as starting material, a cell bank system must be established with documentation of genetic stability, identity by sequencing, and integrity testing.
rules.source_section: 6.3.1.1 Quality of linear DNA derived from plasmid as starting material
rules.verbatim_quote: A cell bank system should be established for plasmid DNA production. Testing should include genetic stability, identity by sequencing, and integrity of the plasmid DNA.

rules.rule_text: Linear DNA starting material must be tested for residual genomic DNA, RNA, protein, sterility, and endotoxin prior to use in mRNA manufacture.
rules.source_section: 6.3.1.1 Quality of linear DNA derived from plasmid as starting material
rules.verbatim_quote: Testing of linear DNA should include residual genomic DNA, RNA, protein, sterility, and endotoxin before use in mRNA production.

rules.rule_text: Process validation is generally not required for candidate mRNA vaccines in preliminary clinical trials; however, critical quality attributes must be defined and in-process controls established during process development.
rules.verbatim_quote: Process validation is not generally required for candidate vaccines in preliminary clinical trials. However, critical quality attributes should be defined and in-process controls established during process development.
rules.exceptions: Applies only to preliminary clinical trials; full process validation required for licensure

rules.rule_text: Product characterization must employ orthogonal methods to fully characterize the mRNA sequence population, including impurities such as residual host-cell proteins, endotoxins, chromosomal DNA, and double-stranded RNA (dsRNA).
rules.verbatim_quote: Orthogonal methods should be used for product characterization. The sequence population, impurities including residual host-cell proteins, endotoxins, chromosomal DNA, and dsRNA should be characterized.

rules.rule_text: LNP properties including particle size, PEG distribution, and zeta potential must be characterized as part of product characterization.
rules.verbatim_quote: LNP properties such as particle size, PEG distribution, and zeta potential should be characterized.

rules.rule_text: Consistency of manufacture must be demonstrated using consecutive batches produced by validated methods, with a comparability protocol required for any manufacturing changes.
rules.verbatim_quote: Consistency of manufacture should be demonstrated using consecutive batches produced by validated methods. A comparability protocol is required for any manufacturing changes.

rules.rule_text: Drug substance (bulk purified mRNA) identity must be confirmed by direct RNA sequencing, RT-PCR, and high-throughput sequencing to verify the full sequence of the mRNA.
rules.verbatim_quote: Identity of the bulk purified mRNA should be confirmed by direct RNA sequencing, RT-PCR, and high-throughput sequencing to verify the full sequence.

rules.rule_text: dsRNA content in the drug substance must be controlled with strict upper limits and measured by chromatographic methods; dsRNA is a critical safety-relevant impurity requiring rigorous control.
rules.verbatim_quote: dsRNA should be controlled with strict upper limits. Chromatographic methods should be used to measure dsRNA, which is a critical safety-relevant impurity.

rules.rule_text: Drug substance release testing must include mRNA integrity, 5' capping efficiency, 3' poly(A) tail length/homogeneity, and quantification method results as physical state and potency-related quality attributes.
rules.verbatim_quote: Release testing should include mRNA integrity, 5' capping efficiency, 3' poly(A) tail, and quantification methods as physical state and potency-related quality attributes.

rules.rule_text: Drug substance safety attributes must include endotoxin and bacterial/fungal sterility testing; the general safety (innocuity) test is no longer required following its discontinuation by WHO in 2018.
rules.verbatim_quote: Safety attributes should include endotoxin and bacterial/fungal sterility. The general safety test (innocuity test) was discontinued by WHO in 2018 and is no longer required.
rules.exceptions: General safety test (innocuity test) explicitly discontinued by WHO in 2018
cross_references: WHO decision to discontinue general safety test

rules.rule_text: An in-house working reference standard for drug substance must be established from a clinically evaluated batch that has been fully characterized.
rules.verbatim_quote: An in-house working reference standard should be established from a clinically evaluated batch that has been fully characterized.

rules.rule_text: Real-time stability studies for the drug substance must be initiated in accordance with WHO Guidelines on stability evaluation of vaccines, with sufficient data to support the proposed shelf life.
rules.verbatim_quote: Real-time stability studies for the drug substance should be initiated in accordance with WHO Guidelines on stability evaluation of vaccines to support the proposed shelf life.
cross_references: WHO Guidelines on stability evaluation of vaccines

rules.rule_text: LNP formation process must be controlled using a Design of Experiments (DoE) approach, with defined specifications for particle size, polydispersity, RNA encapsulation efficiency, and surface properties.
rules.source_section: 6.8 Manufacture and control of final formulated vaccine
rules.verbatim_quote: A DoE approach should be used for LNP formation. Specifications should be defined for particle size, polydispersity, RNA encapsulation efficiency, and surface properties.

rules.rule_text: Hold-time validation for intermediates during drug product manufacture must be performed to support the proposed hold times at defined storage conditions.
rules.source_section: 6.8 Manufacture and control of final formulated vaccine
rules.verbatim_quote: Hold-time validation for intermediates should be performed to support the proposed hold times at defined storage conditions.

rules.rule_text: The aseptic filling process must be validated, and container-closure integrity testing must be performed, with extractables and leachables assessed for the final drug product container.
rules.source_section: 6.8 Manufacture and control of final formulated vaccine
rules.verbatim_quote: Aseptic filling should be validated, and container-closure integrity testing should be performed. Extractables and leachables should be assessed for the final drug product container.

rules.rule_text: For multi-dose vials, a time restriction for use after first opening must be established and validated, with demonstrated microbial safety for the in-use period.
rules.source_section: 6.8 Manufacture and control of final formulated vaccine
rules.verbatim_quote: For multi-dose vials, a time restriction for use after first opening should be established and validated with demonstrated microbial safety for the in-use period.

rules.rule_text: Drug product identity testing must be performed; purity and impurity testing must include dsRNA, nanoparticle size, mRNA-lipid ratio, polydispersity index, and degree of encapsulation as additional quality attributes.
rules.source_section: 6.8 Manufacture and control of final formulated vaccine
rules.verbatim_quote: Identity testing should be performed on the drug product. Additional quality attributes including dsRNA, nanoparticle size, mRNA-lipid ratio, polydispersity index, and degree of encapsulation should be measured.

rules.rule_text: Potency of the final drug product must be assessed by a quantitative validated functional method, using either cell-based transfection assays or cell-free assays; a minimum dose and maximum dose based on human safety data must be agreed with the NRA.
rules.source_section: 6.8 Manufacture and control of final formulated vaccine
rules.verbatim_quote: Potency should be assessed by a quantitative validated functional method using cell-based transfection or cell-free assays. A minimum dose and maximum dose based on human safety data should be agreed with the NRA.

rules.rule_text: Real-time stability studies for the drug product must be performed at the proposed storage conditions, including stability-indicating attributes specific to mRNA vaccines; multi-dose in-use stability must also be demonstrated.
rules.source_section: 6.8 Manufacture and control of final formulated vaccine
rules.verbatim_quote: Real-time stability studies for the drug product should be performed at the proposed storage conditions. Multi-dose in-use stability should be demonstrated.
cross_references: WHO Guidelines on stability evaluation of vaccines

rules.rule_text: Where deep-freeze storage is required, alternative short-term storage conditions must be validated and documented to support cold-chain requirements in developing country settings.
rules.source_section: 6.8 Manufacture and control of final formulated vaccine
rules.verbatim_quote: Where deep-freeze storage is required, alternative short-term storage conditions should be validated to support cold-chain requirements in developing country settings.
rules.exceptions: Required when deep-freeze storage is the primary storage condition

rules.rule_text: Labelling must include the INN, names and addresses of the manufacturer and distributor, lot number, nature and content of drug substance, full composition including excipients, adjuvant statement if applicable, dosage form, immunization schedule, route of administration, and number of doses per container.
rules.verbatim_quote: Labels should include the INN, names and addresses, lot number, nature and content of drug substance, composition including excipients, adjuvant statement, dosage form, immunization schedule, route of administration, and number of doses.

rules.rule_text: Labelling must include preservative name and concentration, antibiotic statement, residuals statement, temperature requirements during storage and transport, container-closure system description, expiry/retest date, dosing schedules, special handling instructions for RNase exposure, and contraindications/warnings.
rules.verbatim_quote: Labels should include preservative name and concentration, antibiotic statement, residuals, temperature for storage and transport, container-closure, expiry date, dosing schedules, special handling for RNase exposure, and contraindications/warnings.

rules.rule_text: Nonclinical evaluation of mRNA vaccines must follow WHO guidelines on nonclinical evaluation of vaccines and WHO guidelines on nonclinical evaluation of vaccine adjuvants and adjuvanted vaccines.
rules.verbatim_quote: Nonclinical evaluation of mRNA vaccines should be conducted in accordance with WHO guidelines on nonclinical evaluation of vaccines (ref 18) and WHO Guidelines on nonclinical evaluation of vaccine adjuvants and adjuvanted vaccines (ref 19).
cross_references: WHO guidelines on nonclinical evaluation of vaccines (WHO/BS/03.1969)
WHO Guidelines on nonclinical evaluation of vaccine adjuvants

rules.rule_text: Nonclinical pharmacology studies must evaluate durability of immune responses and innate immune responses triggered by RNA and type I interferon induction.
rules.verbatim_quote: Pharmacology studies should evaluate the durability of immune responses and innate immune responses including RNA and type I interferon induction.
cross_references: WHO guidelines on nonclinical evaluation of vaccines

rules.rule_text: Nonclinical safety/toxicity studies must assess biodistribution and persistence of mRNA and LNP components, including inflammation from mRNA and LNP innate immune sensor activation, unexpected toxicities from modified nucleosides, and genotoxicity and systemic toxicity of novel lipids.
rules.verbatim_quote: Safety studies should assess biodistribution and persistence, inflammation from mRNA and LNP innate immune sensors, unexpected toxicities from modified nucleosides, and genotoxicity and systemic toxicity of novel lipids.

rules.rule_text: Integration risk assessment is explicitly NOT applicable to mRNA vaccines as mRNA does not integrate into the genome; this must not be confused with DNA vaccine requirements.
rules.verbatim_quote: Integration risk is not applicable to mRNA vaccines as mRNA does not integrate into the genome.
rules.exceptions: This exclusion applies only to mRNA vaccines; DNA vaccines still require integration risk assessment
cross_references: WHO Guidelines on nonclinical evaluation of DNA vaccines

rules.rule_text: Developmental toxicity studies are required for mRNA vaccines when the target population includes women of childbearing potential; existing clinical platform data may support abbreviating these studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: Developmental toxicity studies are required if the target population includes women of childbearing potential. Clinical platform data may support abbreviating these studies.
rules.exceptions: May be abbreviated if clinical platform data from the same LNP/mRNA platform exists

rules.rule_text: During a public health emergency, an abbreviated nonclinical programme is acceptable: if the same antigen sequence change is made on an established platform, only an immunogenicity/challenge-protection study is required; a novel antigen on clinically tested LNPs may require full toxicology.
rules.source_section: 7.3 Accelerating nonclinical evaluation during public health emergency
rules.verbatim_quote: During a public health emergency, an abbreviated programme may be acceptable. If the same antigen sequence change is made on an established platform, only an immunogenicity/challenge-protection study is required. A novel antigen on clinically tested LNPs may require full toxicology.
rules.exceptions: Applies only during declared public health emergencies; both novel antigen AND novel LNP requires the most extensive nonclinical package

rules.rule_text: Clinical evaluation of mRNA vaccines must adhere to WHO GCP guidelines and WHO Guidelines on Clinical Evaluation of Vaccines (TRS924 Annex1).
rules.verbatim_quote: Clinical evaluation should adhere to WHO GCP guidelines and WHO Guidelines on clinical evaluation of vaccines (TRS924 Annex 1).

rules.rule_text: Safety monitoring must include surveillance for transient lymphocyte decreases (Grades 1–3), reactogenicity post-vaccination, AESI surveillance, anaphylaxis monitoring, and myocarditis/pericarditis monitoring.
rules.verbatim_quote: Safety monitoring should include transient lymphocyte decreases Grades 1–3, reactogenicity post-vaccination, AESI surveillance, anaphylaxis monitoring, and myocarditis/pericarditis monitoring.

rules.rule_text: mRNA vaccines typically induce a Th1-type immune response bias; clinical immunogenicity evaluation must characterize the type and scope of immune responses, including both humoral and cellular responses.
rules.verbatim_quote: mRNA vaccines typically induce a Th1-type immune response bias. Clinical immunogenicity evaluation should characterize the type and scope of immune responses including humoral and cellular responses.

rules.rule_text: Total mRNA dose and LNP dose must be considered when evaluating safety in clinical trials; booster evaluation must be included in clinical development to assess repeat-dose safety and immunogenicity.
rules.verbatim_quote: Total mRNA dose and LNP dose should be considered when evaluating safety. Booster evaluation should be included in clinical development to assess repeat-dose safety and immunogenicity.

rules.rule_text: Active pharmacovigilance plans and post-introduction effectiveness studies are required as part of the clinical development and post-authorization requirements for mRNA vaccines.
rules.verbatim_quote: Active pharmacovigilance plans and post-introduction effectiveness studies are required for mRNA vaccines.

rules.rule_text: International standards measured in International Units (IU) must be used for immunogenicity assay standardization across clinical sites to enable inter-laboratory and inter-study comparability.
rules.verbatim_quote: International standards in International Units (IU) should be used for immunogenicity assay standardization to enable inter-laboratory and inter-study comparability.

rules.rule_text: Efficacy evaluation for mRNA vaccines must be disease-specific; the use of placebo must be subject to special ethical consideration, particularly for COVID-19 vaccines where established vaccines exist.
rules.verbatim_quote: Efficacy evaluation should be disease-specific. The use of placebo is subject to special ethical consideration, particularly for COVID-19 vaccines where established vaccines exist.
rules.exceptions: Placebo use requires special ethical justification when licensed vaccines are available for the same disease

rules.rule_text: For variant vaccines derived from an established mRNA platform, immunobridging from the original to the variant candidate vaccine using comparative antibody titres is acceptable on a per-case basis during public health emergencies.
rules.source_section: 8.3 Efficacy evaluation during public health emergency with variants
rules.verbatim_quote: Immunobridging from the original to the variant candidate vaccine using comparative antibody titres is acceptable on a per-case basis during public health emergencies.
rules.exceptions: Applies on a per-case basis during declared public health emergencies only; requires NRA agreement

rules.rule_text: Records of all manufacturing operations including in-process controls and testing must be maintained and retained in accordance with GMP requirements.
rules.verbatim_quote: Records of all manufacturing operations including in-process controls and testing should be maintained and retained in accordance with GMP requirements.

rules.rule_text: Retained samples of each lot of drug substance and drug product must be kept for a minimum period beyond the expiry date to allow for investigation of quality complaints.
rules.verbatim_quote: Retained samples of each lot of drug substance and drug product should be kept for a minimum period beyond the expiry date to allow for investigation of quality complaints.

rules.rule_text: Distribution and transport conditions must be validated to ensure the mRNA vaccine maintains quality and potency throughout the cold chain from manufacturer to point of use.
rules.verbatim_quote: Distribution and transport conditions should be validated to ensure the mRNA vaccine maintains quality and potency throughout the cold chain from manufacturer to point of use.

rules.rule_text: For starting materials released for mRNA manufacture, a formal starting material release step must be defined and documented, with acceptance criteria established before material is used in the manufacturing process.
rules.source_section: 6.3.2 Release of starting and raw materials
rules.verbatim_quote: A formal starting material release step should be defined and documented, with acceptance criteria established before material is used in the manufacturing process.
